Based on my understanding and the behavior I observed, hierarchy (especially drill-down behavior) is only fully supported in SAP Analytics Cloud when using an Import Connection.

In case of Live Connection from S/4HANA CDS views, even though the hierarchy is visible, it does not behave as a proper drill-down hierarchy in SAC.

entry.title: Upcoming Customer Engagement Initiative: AI-Assisted Guidance in UI Theme Designer
entry.content:
With the growing adoption of large language models and conversational AI, it is increasingly important that these technologies effectively understand and support user needs within the UI Theme Designer. This customer engagement initiative explores how SAP Joule and other conversational AI tools can simplify theming workflows, improve usability, and deliver meaningful, context-aware guidance to users.

Together with participants, we aim to:

  • Enhance User Experience
    Enable faster, more intuitive theme creation through AI-driven guidance
  • Evaluate AI Effectiveness
    Assess how well AI understands user intent and delivers accurate, relevant support
  • Improve Documentation Quality
    Identify gaps and enhance clarity, completeness, and accessibility
  • Identify Future Opportunities
    Discover high-impact AI use cases within theming workflows

Key activities will include user scenario testing, hands-on evaluation of SAP Joule and similar tools, user feedback collection, documentation review, and performance analysis. Findings will be translated into concrete, actionable improvements.

Timeline

  • Registration open: until June 21
  • Initial Kick-off Call: early September
  • Deep Dive Sessions: October – November

Registration Link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/4230 

We look forward to your participation and to shaping the future of AI-assisted theming together!

entry.title: Crystal Reports runtime application to Crystal Reports 2025 with .NET VB.NET runtime only deployment
entry.content:
We are migrating a large Crystal Reports runtime application to Crystal Reports 2025 (.NET VB.NET runtime only deployment).

Architecture requirements:

  • ~900 reports

  • SQL Server backend

  • runtime-only deployment (no Crystal Designer installed on client machines)

  • reports contain subreports and stored procedures

  • dynamic server/database/user/password passed at runtime

  • reports currently use OLE DB (ADO) with MSOLEDBSQL

  • SQL Authentication

  • CrystalReportViewer preview and PDF export support

Current runtime logic:

  • ReportDocument.Load()

  • DataSourceConnections.SetConnection()

  • Table.ApplyLogOnInfo()

  • subreport table logons also applied recursively

Observed behaviour:

  • tbl.TestConnectivity() succeeds for main report and subreports

  • runtime datasource logging shows updated server/database/user correctly

  • HOWEVER CrystalReportViewer preview and ExportToDisk() still fail with:
    "Database logon failed"

  • VerifyDatabase() also fails

  • Crystal appears to re-open saved datasource metadata internally during render/export

Question:
What is the SAP-recommended architecture for Crystal Reports 2025 when reports must dynamically connect to different SQL Servers/databases at runtime?

Specifically:

  1. Is full runtime datasource identity rebinding officially supported/reliable with OLE DB (ADO) + MSOLEDBSQL?

  2. Is Crystal expected to require a stable saved datasource identity even when runtime logons are applied?

  3. Is the recommended approach:

    • fixed datasource identity + runtime credentials only
      OR

    • full datasource/server/database rebinding?

  4. Are there known Crystal 2025 runtime limitations where:

    • TestConnectivity succeeds

    • but ExportToDisk / CrystalReportViewer render still fails?

  5. Is there a preferred provider/connection method for dynamic runtime rebinding:

    • OLE DB (ADO) + MSOLEDBSQL

    • ODBC

    • native SQL Server driver

    • another supported approach?

entry.title: AI-Assisted Error Resolution for Product Costing Errors
entry.content:
Introduction and Background

Following blog Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur, I would like to introduce how AI can assist you to resolve product cost posting error. The AI-Assisted Error Resolution solution consists of three integrated capabilities that work together to create a seamless error resolution experience.

Situation Handling: Proactively notifies cost accountants when errors occur from event-based production cost postings. This part is covered in detail in a companion blog post by my colleague: "Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur".

Error Explanation: Joule explains to the cost accountant why the error occurred, providing clear root cause analysis.

Step-by-Step Guided Resolution: Joule provides text-based resolution guidance to help the cost accountant resolve the error and proceed with reposting, recommending the best option when multiple resolution paths exist.

End-to-End Process Flow

The process flow can be started in two ways:

Case 1: Starting from a Situation on My Home

  1. A situation is triggered when an event-based order posting encounters an error
  2. The cost accountant receives a situation notification on their SAP Fiori Home page
  3. Clicking on the situation opens the Situation Page with error details
  4. The cost accountant opens Joule and asks for explanation of the error for a specific order
  5. Joule explains the root cause and proposes different resolutions with a recommended option
  6. The cost accountant follows Joule's step-by-step instructions to resolve the error

Case 2: Directly Asking Joule

  1. The cost accountant opens Joule and prompts: "Show me my errors in company code XYZ"
  2. Joule shows the error list based on the user prompt
  3. The user selects one of the items to get an explanation from Joule
  4. Joule explains the root cause and proposes different resolutions with a recommended option
  5. The cost accountant follows Joule's instructions to resolve the error step by step

AI-Assisted Error Resolution — Detailed Solution

In the companion blog "Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur", we covered how Situation Handling proactively notifies cost accountants when event-based posting errors occur. In this section, we dive deeper into the AI-assisted capabilities: how Joule explains errors and guides users through resolution.

Starting from CE2602, the Event-Based Production Costs solution (Scope Item 3F0) supports AI-assisted error resolution through Joule. Once cost accountants are notified of errors via situations, they can leverage Joule to understand the root cause and receive guided resolution steps.

Case 1: Explain Error by Joule (Start from Situation Page)

When a cost accountant receives a situation notification and navigates to the Situation Page, they can see all the affected orders with error details. From there, they can open Joule and ask for an error explanation for a specific order.

How it works:

  1. Navigate to the Situation Page where all affected orders are displayed with error details (message, company code, message class, order number, etc.)
  2. Open Joule and ask for the error explanation for a specific order [ Order number] via a prompt
  3. Joule analyzes the error and provides a clear explanation of the root cause
  4. Joule proposes different resolution options with a recommended option highlighted
  5. Right after the recommended resolution, relevant apps are proposed for proceeding to the next step (e.g., "Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing", "Schedule Product Costing Jobs")

Figure 1: Situation Page with Joule providing error explanation and resolution options

Figure 2: Joule recommends the best resolution option and proposes relevant apps for reposting

As shown above, Joule not only explains the error but also provides actionable resolution steps with a clear recommendation. The cost accountant can then directly open the suggested app to proceed with reposting.

Case 2: Joule Shows Error List Based on User Prompt

If cost accountants want to know about existing errors in their company code directly — without navigating through an active situation — they can simply ask Joule.

How it works:

  1. Open Joule and ask: "Show me errors in company code [XXXX]" (company code must be specified)
  2. Joule shows the error list based on the user prompt; by default it displays 6 items with a direct "Explain Error" button for each
  3. The user can click "Explain Error" on any item to get Joule's root cause analysis and resolution guidance
  4. The user can also click "View More" to see additional errors in Joule, or "Open in App" to navigate to the Manage Product Costing Errors app for full error management

Figure 3: Joule displays the error list with "Explain Error" buttons and the Manage Product Costing Errors app in the background

Figure 4: Error list in Joule showing orders with errors, each with an "Explain Error" action button

Case 3: Joule Shows Situation List Based on User Prompt

For cost accountants who want to get an overview of all their active situations, they can ask Joule to show their situation list directly.

How it works:

  1. Open Joule and prompt: "Show me my situations"
  2. Joule shows the situation list with details including description, status, and age
  3. Each situation has a "Show Details" button for deeper investigation
  4. The user can also click on the "My Situations – Extended" app link to view the full situation overview in the dedicated app

Figure 5: Joule displays the situation list with "Show Details" buttons

Figure 6: My Situations – Extended app with Joule side panel showing situation details

Additional Information

Limitations

  • The explanation result supports English language only with release CE2602 delivery. This limitation will be removed in a later release.
  • In release CE2602, there is no direct integration between situation and Joule — the cost accountant needs to open Joule separately and ask for the error explanation. This limitation will be removed in a later release.

Useful Links

  • SAP Help Portal – Production Accounting
  • Video in discovery center: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, error resolution for cost accounting

Related Blog

This blog is part of a two-part series on AI-assisted error handling for product costing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: "Stay Informed When Product Cost Posting Errors Occur" — Covers situation handling setup and notifications.

entry.title: Re: Establish Connection Between SAC and On-Premise HANA DB
entry.content:
Hi,

 

If you are using Live Tunnel connection to SAP HANA which leverage SAP Cloud Connector to transfer the HTTPS request from SAC to SAP HANA, please ensure that you have configured SAP Cloud Connector correctly:

- You have configured the subaccount of SAC tenant in Cloud Connector

- You have configured system mapping and accessible resource correctly

Refer to:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/3bdb65253c8046b2b8234c554072569f.html

Before using SSO authentication, it is strongly recommended to establish Live Tunnel connection using basic auth firstly.


Best regareds,
Xiwen

entry.title: Re: No Successor for FOR FIORI APP id F3556 F3557
entry.content:
Hello,

Looks like the BC-CCM-DAG team already shared information about your concern.

Please read the note 2869647-Guidance for use of Data Aging in SAP S/4HANA.
As described in the note, for application data, we find that customers’ intended access to data is very often incompatible with the performance characteristics of aged data.
Section Further Technical Considerations in this note, talks about the other methodologies that can be used instead of data aging.

There will be no further developments in data aging. We are providing the support to our existing customer for data aging.

Best regards,
Joe

entry.title: Re: SAC reports to live HANA prompting for user name and...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I believe that you are using Live Direct connection to SAP HANA using basic authentication, which is designed to prompt credential everytime.

If you want to avoid such credential prompt, you need to enable single sign-on (SSO)  for the live connection with the following Prerequisites met:

  • If you want users to have a single sign-on experience to your data, check that you are using the same Identity Provider (IdP) for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP HANA. For more information on setting up your identity provider in SAP Analytics Cloud, see Enable a Custom SAML Identity Provider (Legacy Custom IdP).

  • Ensure that all users are SAML configured.

  • Ensure that the InA package (/sap/bc/ina/service/v2) or a higher-level package is configured for SAML authentication. For details, see the SAP HANA XS Classic Configuration Parameters.

 

Refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/58c890e1c89d41e69b2cec31bac2d95f.html 

 

Best regards,
Xiwen

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - External logging to Datadog Using OpenTelemetry
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) with Software version update 8.42.* has introduced support for OpenTelemetry (OTEL) as an external logging destination, allowing you to stream Message Processing Logs (MPL) directly to OTEL-supported observability platfroms like Datadog, Grafana, Splunk etc for centralized observability.

What is OpenTelemetry? OpenTelemetry is a vendor-neutral framework for collecting, processing, and exporting telemetry data—metrics, logs, and traces. It's widely adopted across the industry and supported by most observability platforms, including Datadog. This standardized approach means your integration logs can be correlated with other systems and applications in your Datadog environment.

In this guide, I will walk through the step-by-step configuration needed to send CPI logs to Datadog using the OTEL integration, including prerequisites, BTP setup, and validation of the data flow.

Prerequisites

Before you begin, ensure you have the following:

  • An active Datadog account with access to the organization settings
  • A Datadog API key (available at Organization Settings > API Keys)
  • BTP subaccount with Connectivity and Destination access
  • Acess in BTP subaccount to create/update service instance of Process Integration Runtime with appropriate roles

Step 1: Configure Your Datadog Setup

First, prepare your Datadog environment to receive logs from CPI.

Get Your API Key

Navigate to your Datadog organization settings and create an API key if you don't have one already. Go to Organization Settings and select API Keys. Create a new key and copy it—you'll need this for the BTP destination configuration.



Identify Your Datadog Region and OTEL Endpoint

Depending on where your Datadog organization is hosted, you'll use a different endpoint. For example:

  • EU region: https://otlp.datadoghq.eu/v1/logs
  • US region: https://otlp.datadoghq.com/v1/logs

Confirm your organization's base URL (it appears in your browser when you log in) and append the OTEL log endpoint accordingly.

Step 2: Create the BTP Destination

Now configure the HTTP destination in SAP BTP that will serve as the bridge between CPI and Datadog.

Navigate to Destinations

Log into SAP BTP, open your integration subaccount, and navigate to Connectivity > Destinations.

Create a New Destination

Click "Create Destination" and configure it with the following parameters:

Name: CloudIntegration_MonitoringDataConsumer
Type: HTTP
Authentication: No Authentication
URL: https://otlp.datadoghq.eu/v1/logs

Authentication set to "No Authentication"?  Yes—the Datadog API key will be passed as a header, not as basic auth.

Add Additional Properties

Scroll down and add the following additional properties. These fields tell Datadog how to classify and organize your CPI logs:

Property Name Value Purpose

OTELServiceNameMessageProcessingLogsIdentifies the service sending logs in Datadog
OTELScopeNameMPLLogical grouping identifier for CPI logs
OTELScopeVersion1.0.0Version of the log schema
URL.headers.dd-api-key<Your Datadog API Key>Authentication header for Datadog ingestion
TargetSystemOTELSpecifies OpenTelemetry as the target system

Important: Replace <Your Datadog API Key> with the actual API key you created earlier. Store this key securely and never commit it to version control.

After entering all properties, click Save.

Step 3: Activate External Logging in CPI

With the destination configured, you now need to activate external logging at the tenant level.

Create a Service Instance

In your BTP subaccount, go to Services > Instances and Subscriptions. Create a new instance of Process Integration Runtime with the following settings:

  • Service: Process Integration Runtime
  • Plan: api
  • Roles: ExternalLoggingActivate, ExternalLoggingActivationRead

Once created, generate a service key and copy the following credentials:

  • Client ID
  • Client Secret
  • Token URL
  • API Host (base URL for your CPI tenant)

Authenticate and Call the Activation API

Use Postman (or curl) to authenticate with the token URL and then call the external logging activation endpoint. Here's the flow:

1. Get an OAuth token:

POST {TokenURL}
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials
&client_id={ClientID}
&client_secret={ClientSecret}

Copy the access_token from the response.

2. Activate external logging:

POST https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/activateExternalLogging?defaultLogLevel=INFO
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}

The defaultLogLevel parameter controls which log levels are sent. Options are:

  • NONE—disable external logging
  • INFO—send info, warn, and error logs (recommended)
  • ERROR—send error logs only

A successful response confirms external logging is now active. You should see logs flowing to Datadog within a few seconds.

Verify Activation Status

To confirm external logging is active, query the status endpoint:

GET https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/ExternalLoggingActivationStatus('{TenantName}')
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}

A successful response will show the activation status as true.

Step 4: Verify Data Flow and View Logs in Datadog

Once external logging is activated, CPI will begin sending Message Processing Logs to Datadog in real-time.

Querying Logs in Datadog

In Datadog, open the Logs section and search for logs with the service you configured in BTP Destination OTELServiceName.

service:MessageProcessingLogs

You should see MPL entries streaming into Datadog logs.

Parsing MPL Content as JSON

CPI sends MPL logs in a structured format, but the log content itself may be nested. To extract individual MPL attributes and create reports or dashboards, you can use Datadog's Pipelines feature:

Option 1: JSON Parser — If your log body is valid JSON, use the JSON parser to flatten the structure and extract fields. 

Option 2: GROK Parser — For more complex log formats, use a GROK parser pattern to extract specific fields.

Note: My Datadog was missing JSON parser, so I created 2 GROK Parsing rule as below after carfully verifing all the log content format ingested by SAP CI for various combination (ApplicationMessageId, SenderId, CustomStatus, LastError, LastErrorModelStepId etc) to parse all MPL logs.


Once parsed, you can facet logs by integration name, message ID, status, and other attributes to build dashboards, set up alerts, and troubleshoot issues faster.

Maintenance and Troubleshooting

Updating the Destination

If you need to modify the destination (e.g., change the API key or region), follow this sequence:

  1. Call the deactivateExternalLogging endpoint
  2. Update the destination in BTP
  3. Call the activateExternalLogging endpoint again

Skipping deactivation can result in not reflect the destinatinn updates.

Known Limitations

When CPI sends logs via OTEL, the timeUnixNano and observedTimeUnixNano fields are currently set to 0 instead of a proper Unix epoch value for custom headers. Datadog doesn't process these malformed timestamps, so log ingestion still works but only for Run Events and not Custom Headers. I believe SAP will address this in a future update.

Useful API Endpoints

For reference, here are other relevant endpoints for managing external logging:

Deactivate external logging:
POST https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/deactivateExternalLogging

View external logging events:
GET https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/ExternalLoggingEvents

Check MPL logs (alternative query):
GET https://{TenantName}.{host}.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs

For detailed API specifications, refer to the SAP API Hub documentation on External Logging.

entry.title: Re: Considerations when implementing IAS for SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
@taylor_mcdonald71 

 

I would still challenge your HR and onboarding teams with the following?

Why are they not completing the ONB process? Is it too long cumbersome? If they are not completing the process then are they skipping any compliance aspects, what does your compliance and legal team think about this?  If they do not complete the process how do they get the correct data for EC?  This is pushing work on HR & IT?  I would also be really clear to hires that we need their details so we can get them set up and ready to pay them!  You really need to push back on this, the challenges you are facing are not in IT but in poor recruitment and ONB governance!

There is one thing that may help.  Your design principle seems that you replicate on their hire date which is good.  I would add in another filter and say ‘Hiring is not complete’ flag which mean they will only go when there is a complete EC record. As a result the ONB record will not replicate on their hire date.  What this would mean is if they have not finished the process they can log in and finish their ONB process off on their first couple of days.  This would solve lots of your IAS issue caused by poor Recruitment and ONB governance.

entry.title: Re: Power BI connectivity to SAP
entry.content:
Hi  Team,

I do NOT see any attachments and what is the error message you are getting .

Please follow below steps to connect Power BI to HANA:

  1. Install Latest HDB client software in system where you want to connect Hana Database. Hana client software enables the ODBC Driver as below. 
  2. Type ODBC in windows search bar and click ODBC data source 32/64 bit.
  3. Click add on Database.
  4. Double click on HDBODBC.
  5. Enter below details and save the connection.
    Host: Hana database host
    Instance number as example 00
    And tenant database would be Hana system name
  6. Go to power BI and go to Hana database connection.
  7. Enter username and password of SAP Hana Database 


Refer the below Knowledge Base Articles : 

3623376 - How to connect Power BI to HANA DB

2882465 - When using Power BI to connect to HANA DB using ODBC DSN, an authentication error is generated

Hope this is helpful.

 

~Gaurav

entry.title: Charts / KPI Visualization with RAP Custom Entities
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a RAP application using custom entities, where both entities are implemented through the same ABAP class. The association between these entities is working as expected, and I have successfully handled filtering and paging as well.

At the moment, I can display the List Report, Object Page, and Line Item Reference navigation without any issues.

However, I would now like to enhance the main page by adding visualizations such as charts or KPI-style data representations based on the aggregated data.

I have explored several resources, but I haven’t been able to find a clear or reliable approach for achieving chart/KPI visualization directly using custom entities in RAP.

Is there any supported way or recommended approach to implement such visualizations in this scenario?

Any guidance or suggestions would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Custom Logic to Implement Flexible Billing Document Numbering
entry.content:
Hey

Very useful blog! I did the same for the Distribution Channel, created a custom field and use the data transfer to fill it. 

Important note: After publishing the Badi, you still have to perform 2 customizing steps:

1) Define custom routines for data transfer to Billing Documents (as described)

2) Important to define the copying control!! For example, if you define the routine for the copy from debit memo request to debit note, you must use the config: Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Billing Document, for DR to L2, item category L2N.

Once you maintained that, it will work.

entry.title: What's Hybrid Specification Management?
entry.content:
With the May release, SAP IPD Specification Management is now integrated with SAP S/4HANA Specification Management with a hybrid solution, where customers can store all their specification data in their SAP S/4HANA system, but still use the features of SAP IPD, for example, but not limited to: 

  • Supplier collaboration within the system utilizing the SAP Ariba integration
  • Comparing specifications for informed decisions
  • Easily find specifications with AI support

Here is a video overview of the functionality:

 

More Information

You can find a more detailed description on the Help Portal: Hybrid Specification Management | SAP Help Portal and its subpages.

If you are interested in what needs to be configured before using the scenario, see: Integrating with SAP S/4HANA Specification Management | SAP Help Portal and its subpages.

What's Next?

Stay tuned for a more detailed overview on the configuration tasks that need to be done before the functionality can be used.

About the Author

Zsofia is a development manager at SAP IPD, working in the PLM area for 10+ years.

entry.title: New SAP Data Architecture Learning Journey & SAP Certified Data Architect Certification is now Live
entry.content:
TLDR: The Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Learning Journey is now live on learning.sap and alongside it the New Role Play based SAP Certified Data Architect certification is now available in this learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-a-certified-sap-data-architect

Early in my career, I sat in a room full of architects debating a major platform decision. The conversation covered integration patterns, application landscapes, and total cost of ownership. No one discussed the data. Not the quality of it, not how it would flow, not how it would be governed. We treated data as the responsibility of someone else (master data team and BW team) in a different meeting. Years later, we are seeing AI initiatives slow down because of untrustworthy data, I understand exactly what was missing from that room.

Data architecture has spent years as a supporting discipline important, but rarely the headline. That has changed. The shift to agentic AI has moved data architecture from a back-office concern to a front-line competitive capability.

Agentic AI systems do not simply retrieve information. They reason, plan, and act. They require data that is real-time, contextually grounded, semantically rich, and governed with precision. An agent operating on stale, fragmented, or poorly modelled data does not just produce mediocre outputs it can produce incorrect actions. At enterprise scale, that is a governance and business risk problem, not just a technical one.

The architects who will lead in this environment are those who can hold both conversations at once: the strategic (how data drives business value) and the technical (how to design the platforms, integration patterns, and governance structures that make that value real).

That is exactly what we have tried to address with the New Data Architecture learning Course (Advanced) and a New Data Architect Certification from SAP

Introducing the Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Course

 

The Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Learning Journey (course BDCDA2) is now live on learning.sap.com. Some of you might have done ‘Becoming a Data Architect – Foundational Course’ which was launched last year and this new course takes a deeper look at the topics related to Data Architecture.

Across four-unit journey designed for architects who are ready to move beyond foundational concepts and into the architecture decisions, framework synthesis, and platform-specific depth that define effective data leadership in the agentic AI era.

  1.  Unit 1: Architecting Data Strategy for the Agentic AI Era

The journey opens with strategy, because that is where most data initiatives fail first. Unit 1 builds the five foundational pillars of data strategy: Governance, Data literacy, Stewardship, Architecture, and Technology enablement and connects each directly to AI readiness. It examines what agentic AI demands from data pipelines that traditional analytics architectures were not built to provide: real-time streaming, semantic enrichment, and contextual grounding. It then moves to leadership and culture, the organizational factors that determine whether a data strategy survives contact with reality and closes with a framework for measuring data strategy success and analyzing real-world value patterns.

  1. Unit 2: Integrating Frameworks for Data Architecture

Architecture without a framework produces inconsistency. Unit 2 examines the three frameworks that define the professional standard in this field: TOGAF, DAMA-DMBOK, and the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework and, guides how to synthesize them rather than apply them in isolation. It then applies that synthesis to two of the most significant architectural paradigms in the market today: Data Mesh and Data Fabric.

  1.  Unit 3: Harmonizing SAP Business Data Cloud Within the Open Data Ecosystem

Unit 3 is where strategy and frameworks meet the SAP platform. It begins with BDC Connect and the Delta Sharing protocol the mechanism that enables zero-copy data sharing across ecosystem boundaries and then works through each major integration scenario in the open data ecosystem: Databricks, Snowflake (including all three connectivity options), Microsoft Fabric, and Google Big Query. The unit closes with a dedicated lesson on data federation performance optimization the practical trade-offs between federation, replication, and zero-copy sharing that every architect advising on SAP Business Data Cloud deployments needs to understand cold.

  1. Unit 4: Designing Advanced Data Models and Architecture for SAP Business Data Cloud

The final unit addresses SAP Business Data Cloud as a Business Data Fabric how it implements federation, replication, and zero-copy sharing to connect heterogeneous data sources while preserving business context and semantic coherence. It covers Data Mesh in SAP BDC, including how Datasphere Spaces implement the four Data Mesh principles in practice. It then moves to one of the most consequential topics in enterprise AI architecture: semantic models and knowledge graphs as the foundation for trustworthy AI and RAG-based systems.

 Introducing the SAP Certified Data Architect Certification

Alongside the learning journey, we have launched the SAP Certified Data Architect certification.

The SAP Certified Data Architect is an AI role-play-based certification that assesses how well candidates understand modern data architecture in the context of strategic business success. It covers the five major architectural paradigms: Data Warehouse, Data Lake, Data Mesh, Data Fabric, and Data Lakehouse, alongside the core elements of data maturity: governance frameworks, data security, and business-aligned data strategy. It uses interactive question-and-answer format, assessing the understanding of SAP Data Architecture in the agentic AI era specifically the ability to synthesize and articulate how SAP Business Data Cloud enables governed, semantically coherent data access across hybrid cloud environments.

Note: It is recommended that the candidate completes the 1) Becoming an SAP Data Architect and 2) Mastering SAP Data Architecture courses before attempting the certificate to ensure successful completion.

 Who This Is For

The learning journey and certification are designed for practitioners who are already working in the data and architecture space and want to develop the rigor, depth, and formal recognition to operate at senior level. That includes data architects designing enterprise data platforms, enterprise architects expanding their data architecture competency, analytics and BI leaders responsible for governed data access, and technical decision-makers advising customers on SAP Business Data Cloud strategy.

 If you are starting your data architecture journey, the foundational 'Becoming a Data Architect' learning journey on learning.sap.com is the right starting point. The advanced journey picks up where that one ends.

Getting Started

  1. The Becoming a Certified SAP Data Architect Learning Journey live now for both internal and external learners: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-a-certified-sap-data-architect
  2. SAP Certified Data Architect certification registration: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-data-architect-sap-business-data-cloud

A Final Thought

In the agentic AI era, the constraint on enterprise intelligence is rarely the algorithm. It is the architecture underneath it the decisions made about how data is structured, governed, connected, and made meaningful. Those decisions require a specific set of skills. This learning journey and certification exist to build them.

The practitioners who invest in that depth now will be the ones designing the data foundations that AI-enabled enterprises run on. That work is not infrastructure. It is strategy.

Happy Learning!

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core ( Coimbatore, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, June 17th, 2026 from 10:00 - 15:30 IST in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address:  Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited, CHIL-SEZ, Keeranatham Village, Coimbatore - 641 035, Tamil Nadu.

The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate @sheenamk

The event is kindly hosted by Bosch Coimbatore. Thank you!

Prerequisites: 

Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Vivaldi, Brave, Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 25 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A confirmation email will be send to all registered participants 1 week before the event.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: SAC Calendar time to complete actions in Parallel
entry.content:
hi,

We have noticed that when 2 Action (Multi Action or Data Action) run at the same time, both will take a lot more time to complete than if they run in sequence (one after the other).

This happens even if each is impacting a different models (i.e. one updating FINANCE and one updating REV_PLANNING).

The same task running in concurrence (at the same time as other task) may take more than to times more to complete.

Is there any way to manage parallel jobs in SAC?

Can we schedule 2 tasks to run at the same time in the calendar and not have this huge impact on time to complete?

entry.title: Issue in Milestone Activity Confirmation at Order Level
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We observed an issue while performing milestone activity confirmation at the production order level.

Issue Description:
When we execute order confirmation with milestone confirmation functionality enabled, the system is confirming only the last operation activity parameters automatically. However, the activity parameters for all previous operations are not getting confirmed.

Expected Behavior:
As per milestone confirmation logic, once the milestone operation is confirmed, the system should automatically confirm all preceding operations along with their respective activity parameters.

Actual Behavior:
Only the last operation activity parameters are getting confirmed, while activity parameters for earlier operations remain unconfirmed.

Test Scenario:

  1. Create a production order with multiple operations.
  2. Maintain milestone confirmation for the last operation.
  3. Release the order.
  4. Perform confirmation at order level for the milestone operation.
  5. Check operation/activity confirmation status.

Observation:<

entry.title: Re: Quality Lot Status Not Changed
entry.content:
Actual Error Screen:

When posting the Goods Issue in VL02N (and other movements as well), the following error is displayed:

Stock data of serial number XXXXXXXXXXXXXX is not suitable for this movement.
Serial number XXXXXXXXXXXXXX cannot be used here.

 

 

Error Reason:
 The below-mentioned lot number has AUTO UD posting enabled. During the automatic UD posting process, an undefined error occurred. As a result, the stock was moved to the unrestricted-use location; however, the lot status is still showing as SPRQ (Stock Postings Not Yet Completed for the lot)

 



This lot have 9 Qty, that Related Serial number is generated.

 

But Serial number Stock type is Showing as 02 – In Quality Inspection, So above given error occurred. 

 

Solution: 1

As per SAP standard not able to Change the Serial number Stock type 02 in to 01 in IQ02 T code.

So go to T code OIS2 – Serial Number Profile – Change the Stock Check Status Error in to Warning .

 

 

 

Then go to T code IQ02 – Change the Stock Type 02 - Quality into 01 – Unrestricted  

 

 
Solution: 2

Directly go to SE16N use the TABLE EQBS and change the Stock type 02 into 01

 

 Now go and check the Stock posting in VL02N – Serial Number Error has been Cleared.
Error Has been Cleared.

entry.title: How SAPPHIRE 2026 directly influences your Cloud ERP Private modernization journey
entry.content:
Every SAP customer is on a different stage of its cloud modernization journey and those who haven’t yet started the discussions (and that could have happened due to a variety of reasons) must go through this blog.

At SAP Sapphire 2026, the conversation changed significantly.

SAP made two important announcements that together create a compelling—and urgent—case for customers running ECC On-Premise (ECC OP), S/4HANA On-Premise (S/4HANA OP), and ERP Private Cloud Edition (ERP PCE) systems to accelerate modernization toward Cloud ERP Private.

The message is becoming increasingly clear: customers who commit to modernization early will gain faster access to innovation, AI, and long-term supportability.

AI Innovation Is No Longer Reserved Only for Full Cloud Customers

One of the most impactful announcements at Sapphire 2026 was SAP’s decision to extend selected AI capabilities to ECC OP and S/4HANA OP customers.

Traditionally, many of SAP’s newest AI-driven innovations—especially those powered through Joule and cloud-native business processes—were positioned primarily for cloud ERP customers.

Now, SAP has opened a new path.

Customers running ECC or S/4HANA On-Premise systems who commit at least 50% of their on-premise maintenance base toward cloud adoption can gain access to selected AI scenarios, even while still operating their OP environments.

Based on the above, it is pertinent to share that cloud systems get all AI capabilities and on-premises systems will get a sub-set. It requires customers and SAP effort to make agents work on-premises, driving our cloud and on-premises differentiation. Note this applies to agents running on ERP systems, not migration agents.

To help accelerate the impact of AI to our customers’ businesses, SAP experts will help enable agentic AI for our new and existing Cloud ERP Private customers by activating up to 3 Joule Assistants in their ERP at no additional cost. Customers can select Joule Assistants that are available in their environments which will support common workflows such as finance, HR and supply chain.

Lastly, it is important to state that there is no change in SAP’s AI policy as AI agents require standardized systems to operate reliably, and the cloud is where SAP can guarantee this foundation, so the cloud remains our strategic focus. The activation of agents on on-premises systems requires services and engineering resources which we are offering to customers that commit to convert at least 50% of their current maintenance fee to the cloud. For those customers, we’ll provide strategic AI use cases so they can begin using and getting value from AI faster. Capabilities may vary based on technical requirements, level of customizations on existing systems, and may require underlying application as required by the agent. Please reach out to your SAP contact for more information.

Essentially, the SAP announcement acknowledges an important market reality:

  • Customers still need to derive innovation value during transition years.
  • AI adoption cannot wait for the “final phase” of ERP transformation.

However, there is an important nuance.

These AI capabilities will still come with boundaries and limitations compared to fully cloud-native environments. The deepest innovation velocity will continue to happen in Cloud ERP Private and public cloud solutions. Which means this announcement should not be interpreted as a reason to delay modernization indefinitely.

Instead, it should be seen as a bridge. A bridge that allows customers to start realizing AI value while they accelerate toward their longer-term cloud ERP architecture.

The ERP PCE Transition Option May Not Apply to Everyone

Another major development shaping customer strategy is the upcoming SAP ERP, private edition, transition option service expected around 2028.

This service is intended to help ERP PCE customers transition more smoothly toward Cloud ERP Private.

At first glance, this sounds reassuring for customers who believe they have more time but there is a critical detail that organizations should not ignore:

The transition option service is expected to include a 2 TB database size criterion.

That means not every ERP PCE customer may qualify and that changes the planning equation entirely.

Organizations that assume they can “wait until 2028” may discover later that:

  • Their system size doesn’t reach the system eligibility criteria
  • Their landscape complexity requires longer preparation cycles
  • Data reduction and archiving activities take years, not months
  • Skilled transformation resources become increasingly constrained as deadlines approach

In other words, the later customers begin, the fewer options they may ultimately have.

ECC and S/4HANA OP Customers Should Treat This as a Strategic Window

The Sapphire 2026 announcements create an interesting transition period.

SAP is effectively giving customers:

  1. Early AI access during the transition journey
  2. A modernization path toward Cloud ERP Private
  3. Incentives to begin cloud commitment now rather than later

Modernization Is No Longer Just About End of Maintenance

For many years, ERP modernization discussions revolved around maintenance deadlines.

That framing is now outdated.

The real differentiator moving forward is innovation velocity.

SAP’s Sapphire 2026 announcements signal a future where AI, automation, and cloud-delivered capabilities become central to enterprise competitiveness—even for customers still operating hybrid landscapes.

But hybrid access is unlikely to be the destination.

It is the transition phase.

And for ECC OP, S/4HANA OP, and ERP PCE customers alike, the smartest move may be to use this phase not as a reason to delay transformation, but as an opportunity to accelerate it thoughtfully and strategically.

entry.title: Re: insert only if not exist
entry.content: How about inserting into the table and check the return code of the INSERT command. If it is error 2601 just ignore and continue to insert more values ?

entry.title: Re: How to Take SAP’s New Performance-Based Certification Exams
entry.content: Hi @SreeAnuj_Nimmala , thanks for reaching out and asking. Let me take the question :). The grading is absolutely comparable and uses the same scoring table and criteria. The main difference is time, AI is much faster (immediate), where as for the expert-based grading we may take up to 20 working days. I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: How to properly logout from a CAP mocked authentication?
entry.content: Thanks Dinu. Just tried and unfortunately it doesn't work. If I user username or both username:password this is just ignored. I event created a unbound function to retrieve the authenticated user and it always gives me 'anonymous' as a result and there is still no way for force the user.

entry.title: Re: Customer-Managed Data Products in SAP BDC: What Really Happens When Your Source Is a Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @VineetBakshi ,

Thank you for the great post on the Databricks integration between SAP and Databricks, really valuable content.

I did want to highlight one nuance: your explanation of the integration is mostly correct, but the part about SAP being the “standard” isn’t entirely accurate. In Databricks, it is possible to share views with SAP Datasphere, as long as the underlying tables are Databricks‑managed and the primary key is defined in the CSN file used to configure the share.

As far as I know, this isn’t possible within Datasphere itself, where only Local Tables can be shared towards Databricks.

Otherwise, an excellent blog, thanks again for putting it together!

entry.title: How to Trace a Role That Appears Blank in PFCG but Still Controls IT0008 Access?
entry.content:
Facing an interesting authorization behavior related to IT0008 maintenance.

There is a particular role which appears completely blank in PFCG. No visible authorization objects, no restrictions, and no obvious configuration linked to infotype access.

However, only users assigned with this role are able to edit IT0008 in PA30 successfully. The moment the role is removed, the system throws an authorization error while trying to maintain the infotype.

Checked:
Standard authorization objects
Structural authorizations
Business roles
Infotype permissions

Guess that the role name itself might be getting checked directly in custom code rather than through standard authorization objects.

Has anyone faced a similar situation where a “blank” role still controlled access, or
the role was hardcoded in custom logic?

What would be the best approach to trace where this role check is happening?

 

Keywords: SAP Authorization, PFCG role, Blank role in PFCG, Role assignment, Authorization check, Authorization error, SAP security

entry.title: Re: How to properly logout from a CAP mocked authentication?
entry.content: Thanks @rotem_puterman-sobe  for the info. However this also does not work. If I use a private window (Edge) using such link it gives me an error "Error occurred when checking if token exchange is needed, or when performing the token exchange". I realized that if I remove the "/login" from the URL (keeping only the BAS link without the "e" parameter) it forces the authentication in BAS (using my company IdP for that) and then I am able to manually add the "/login?e=" part. Still, somehow it just ignores the mock authentication and the popup to request user/password is never shown.

entry.title: Milestone Billing for Service Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: A Complete API-First Guide
entry.content:
Introduction: The Business Need

Consider a common scenario in industrial automation: TechWave Solutions GmbH sells welding robots along with a suite of value-added services — installation, annual technical support, remote diagnostics, and custom training. While the hardware ships through standard stock-based fulfillment, services follow a different commercial rhythm.

Their customer, Apex Manufacturing Ltd., has purchased an Annual Remote Diagnostics Service Package for ¥36,000. Rather than billing the full amount upfront, the contract specifies milestone-based payments:

Milestone Trigger Amount %

1 — Contract SigningUpon SO confirmation¥21,60060%
2 — Service ActivationRemote agent deployed¥14,40040%

This pattern — splitting a service contract into progress-based or event-driven invoices — is milestone billing. It is increasingly common across industries where service delivery spans weeks or months: industrial IoT monitoring, managed infrastructure, phased consulting engagements, and SaaS implementations with go-live milestones.

Why milestone billing matters:

  • Cash flow alignment — Invoice when value is delivered, not all at once
  • Revenue recognition — IFRS 15 / ASC 606 compliance for performance obligations satisfied over time
  • Customer relationship — Reduces upfront payment resistance; ties cost to tangible progress
  • Contractual flexibility — Block invoicing until a milestone is formally accepted

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, milestone billing is implemented through Billing Plans on sales order items. This guide walks you through the complete end-to-end flow using OData APIs — from master data and pricing through to the final billing document. Every step includes copy-pasteable JSON payloads verified against a live S/4HANA Cloud system.

Prerequisites & Configuration Overview

System Landscape

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (tested on release 2502)
  • OData API access via Communication Arrangements:
    • SAP_COM_0109 — Sales Order Integration (API_SALES_ORDER_SRV)
    • SAP_COM_0303 — Billing Document Integration (V4 api_billingdocument)
    • SAP_COM_0116 — Pricing Condition Record (API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV)

Configuration Elements Required

Element App / SSCUI Key Setting

Item CategorySSCUI 100394 — Define Item CategoriesCTAD with Billing Plan Type = 90
Billing Block ReasonsSSCUI 101494 — Define Billing Block Reasons03, Y2, etc.

Note: In S/4HANA Cloud public edition, customizing activities are managed through SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC). Open the "Manage Your Solution" app in Fiori Launchpad to access the CBC portal link, then search for the SSCUI ID (e.g., 100394) to locate and modify the configuration activity.

<!-- SCREENSHOT: "Manage Your Solution" app showing CBC portal link -->

Why Item Category CTAD?

This is the single most important configuration decision. In a standard S/4HANA Cloud system:

  • TAD (default for OR + service material) — does NOT support billing plans (no billing plan type assigned in SSCUI 100394)
  • CTAD — supports billing plan type 90 (milestone billing)
  • TAO / TAM / TAP — do not exist in S/4HANA Cloud public edition

You must explicitly specify SalesOrderItemCategory: "CTAD" when creating the sales order via API. The system will not auto-determine it from item category determination (SSCUI 100393) for this use case.

<!-- SCREENSHOT: SSCUI 100394 — Define Item Categories showing CTAD with Billing Plan Type = 90 -->

Step 1: Sales Order Creation

API Call

 
POST /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder
Content-Type: application/json
 
{
  "SalesOrderType": "OR",
  "SalesOrganization": "1320",
  "DistributionChannel": "20",
  "OrganizationDivision": "00",
  "SoldToParty": "1000260",
  "PurchaseOrderByCustomer": "DIAG-2026-ANNUAL",
  "CustomerPaymentTerms": "0001",
  "IncotermsClassification": "EXW",
  "IncotermsLocation1": "SHANGHAI",
  "RequestedDeliveryDate": "/Date(1778803200000)/",
  "to_Item": [{
    "Material": "LQ_SRV-DIAG",
    "RequestedQuantity": "1",
    "RequestedQuantityUnit": "H",
    "SalesOrderItemCategory": "CTAD"
  }]
}

Critical: You must explicitly set SalesOrderItemCategory: "CTAD". If omitted, the system defaults to TAD, which does not support billing plans.

Response: HTTP 201 — Sales Order number returned (e.g., 588), Net Amount = ¥36,000.00 CNY

Post-Creation: Set Production Plant

The item needs a plant assignment for billing to work. PATCH the item:

 
PATCH /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrderItem(SalesOrder='588',SalesOrderItem='10')
If-Match: *
 
{
  "ProductionPlant": "1320"
}

Response: HTTP 204

<!-- SCREENSHOT: Fiori App "Display Sales Orders" (WebGUI: VA03) — Sales Order overview showing item with CTAD category and ¥36,000 net value -->

Step 2: Billing Plan Configuration

This is the core of milestone billing. The billing plan is a separate entity attached to the sales order item — it cannot be created via deep-insert during SO creation.

Step 2a: Create Billing Plan Header

 
POST /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrderItemBillingPlan
Content-Type: application/json
 
{
  "SalesOrder": "588",
  "SalesOrderItem": "10",
  "BillingPlanType": "90",
  "BillingPlanStartDate": "/Date(1778803200000)/"
}

Key field: BillingPlanType: "90" = Milestone Billing

Response: HTTP 201 — Billing Plan created. Retrieve the plan number:

 
GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrderItemBillingPlan
    ?$filter=SalesOrder eq '588' and SalesOrderItem eq '10'

Returns BillingPlan: "246" (system-assigned number).

Step 2b: Create Milestone Dates

Each milestone is a billing plan item (date line). Create them via:

 
POST /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SlsOrderItemBillingPlanItem
Content-Type: application/json

Milestone #1 — Contract Signing (60%, ¥21,600):

 
{
  "SalesOrder": "588",
  "SalesOrderItem": "10",
  "BillingPlan": "246",
  "BillingPlanDateCategory": "04",
  "BillingPlanBillingDate": "/Date(1778803200000)/",
  "BillingPlanAmount": "21600.00",
  "BillingBlockReason": ""
}

Milestone #2 — Service Activation (40%, ¥14,400):

 
{
  "SalesOrder": "588",
  "SalesOrderItem": "10",
  "BillingPlan": "246",
  "BillingPlanDateCategory": "04",
  "BillingPlanBillingDate": "/Date(1781481600000)/",
  "BillingPlanAmount": "14400.00",
  "BillingBlockReason": "03"
}

Field reference:

Field Value Meaning

BillingPlanDateCategory"04"Milestone date (vs. "01" periodic, "02" partial)
BillingPlanBillingDate/Date(ms)/When this milestone becomes due
BillingPlanAmountdecimalFixed amount for this milestone
BillingBlockReason"" or "03"Empty = ready to bill; "03" = blocked until release

Important: Clear Auto-Assigned Billing Block

The system automatically assigns billing block "Y2" to newly created milestones. You must explicitly clear it for any milestone you want to bill immediately:

 
PATCH /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SlsOrderItemBillingPlanItem
      (SalesOrder='588',SalesOrderItem='10',BillingPlan='246',BillingPlanItem='1')
If-Match: *
 
{
  "BillingBlockReason": ""
}

Response: HTTP 204

Note: This auto-block behavior is system-specific. Your system may use a different default block or none at all, depending on billing plan type configuration.

<!-- SCREENSHOT: Fiori App "Display Sales Orders" (WebGUI: VA03) — Billing Plan tab showing 2 milestone dates with amounts and block status -->

Step 3: Create Billing Document

With Milestone #1 unblocked and its billing date reached, we can now generate the invoice. S/4HANA Cloud uses the OData V4 Billing Document API with the CreateFromSDDocument action.

API Call

 
POST /sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_billingdocument/srvd_a2x/sap/billingdocument/0001/
     BillingDocument/com.sap.gateway.srvd_a2x.api_billingdocument.v0001.CreateFromSDDocument
Content-Type: application/json
 
{
  "_Control": {
    "DefaultBillingDocumentType": "FAZ",
    "AutomPostingToAcctgIsDisabled": true
  },
  "_Reference": [{
    "SDDocument": "0000000588",
    "SDDocumentCategory": "C",
    "BillingDocumentType": "FAZ",
    "BillingDocumentDate": "2026-05-15",
    "DestinationCountry": "CN",
    "SalesOrganization": "1320"
  }]
}

Key points:

  • Billing Type FAZ — This is a Down Payment Request, which is the standard billing document type for milestone billing plans. Do NOT use F2 (standard invoice) — it will fail.
  • SDDocument must be zero-padded to 10 characters
  • SDDocumentCategory: "C" = Sales Order
  • AutomPostingToAcctgIsDisabled: true — Optional; set to false if you want immediate FI posting
  • The API automatically identifies which milestones are eligible (unblocked + date reached) and bills only those

Response: HTTP 200

 
{
  "value": [{
    "BillingDocument": "90000458",
    "BillingDocumentType": "FAZ",
    "TotalNetAmount": 21600.0,
    "TransactionCurrency": "CNY",
    "BillingDocumentDate": "2026-05-15",
    "SoldToParty": "1000260"
  }]
}

The system created billing document 90000458 for exactly ¥21,600 — the 60% milestone amount.

<!-- SCREENSHOT: Fiori App "Display Billing Documents" (WebGUI: VF03) — Billing Document 90000458 showing FAZ type, ¥21,600 net, LQ_SRV-DIAG item -->

Step 4: Verification & Monitoring

Confirm Milestone Status

Read back the billing plan items to verify that Milestone #1 is now closed:

 
GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SlsOrderItemBillingPlanItem
    ?$filter=SalesOrder eq '588' and SalesOrderItem eq '10'

# Date Amount Block Status Meaning

12026-05-15¥21,600—CClosed (billed)
22026-06-15¥14,40003AOpen (blocked)

Releasing Milestone #2

When the service is activated and you're ready to invoice the remaining 40%, simply remove the billing block:

 
PATCH /A_SlsOrderItemBillingPlanItem(SalesOrder='588',SalesOrderItem='10',
       BillingPlan='246',BillingPlanItem='2')
If-Match: *
 
{
  "BillingBlockReason": ""
}

Then call CreateFromSDDocument again — it will generate a second FAZ document for ¥14,400.

Fiori App "Create Billing Documents" (WebGUI: VF04) — Billing Due List

For monitoring across multiple orders, use the Fiori app Create Billing Documents (app ID F0798, or WebGUI transaction VF04):

  • Filter by Sales Documents (check the checkbox)
  • Enter your SD Document number
  • The list shows all eligible billing plan dates with their processing status

<!-- SCREENSHOT: Fiori App "Create Billing Documents" (F0798 / WebGUI: VF04) — Billing Due List showing SO 588 with milestone dates -->

Troubleshooting & Common Pitfalls

Symptom Root Cause Solution

"No billing plan type maintained in sales document item category"Using TAD instead of CTADExplicitly set SalesOrderItemCategory: "CTAD" (verify in SSCUI 100394)
CreateFromSDDocument returns 400 "Check input parameters"Billing date in wrong year (timestamp error)Verify /Date() values — 2026 ≠ 2025!
"Create Billing Documents" app shows 0 results"Sales Documents" checkbox not selected, or billing date filter too narrowCheck both settings
Milestone created with unexpected blockSystem default behavior assigns Y2PATCH BillingBlockReason to ""
"Document is incomplete so billing cannot be carried out"Missing Plant or other required fieldsSet ProductionPlant on the item; ensure delivery date is set
Billing doc created but no FI postingMissing account assignment group (KTGRM) on materialMaintain KTGRM = XB in material master
V2 SD_CUSTOMER_INVOICES_CREATE returns 403API user lacks authorizationThis service requires a separate communication arrangement; use V4 API instead
Billing creates F2 instead of FAZWrong billing type specifiedUse DefaultBillingDocumentType: "FAZ" in the V4 API call

Timestamp Reference

OData V2 uses milliseconds since epoch in UTC:

Date /Date() Value

2026-01-01/Date(1767225600000)/
2026

entry.title: Usage and further actions in ACACTREE02
entry.content:
Attachments to accrual objects

Adding attachments and notes to accrual objects in S/4HANA can be incredibly useful for documentation and reference purposes. This feature allows you to attach documents like EXCEL files or PDFs, along with notes that explain the reasons behind creating the accrual object. Here’s how you can add attachments and notes to an accrual object, using transactions like ACACTREE02 as an example:

Steps to Add Attachments and Notes to an Accrual Object:

1. Navigate to the Accrual Object Display:

o Open the transaction ACACTREE02 to display or manage accrual objects.

o Select the accrual object to which you want to add attachments or notes.

Pause, Suspend and Resume action

Improved Accrual Subobject Management in Display Accrual Object Transaction

In the transaction for displaying Accrual Objects, the following additional functions are now available:

  • Pause Accrual Subobject
  • Suspend Accrual Subobject
  • Resume Accrual Subobject

 

These functions are provided in addition to existing actions:

  • Terminate Accrual Subobject Prematurely
  • Undo Premature Termination

Enhancements with Support Packages and SAP Note 2816562

The usability and features of the actions Pause, Suspend, Resume, and Terminate Accrual Subobject Prematurely have been improved with the following support packages:

  •  Release S/4HANA 1809 SP3
  •  Release S/4HANA 1909 SP1

Key Changes Introduced in These Support Packages (Note 2816562)

New Action: Pause

Pause Action and Status: The Pause action and its status Paused have been introduced.

Changes to the Suspend Action

 Release Posting:

o The Suspend action now performs a release posting. Before this support package, it did not perform the release posting. This is an incompatible change.

o If the release posting is not desired, the Pause action can be used instead.

Suspension Date:

o The Suspend action now requires entering a Suspension Date.

Displaying Suspension and Pause Dates

The Suspension (and Pause) Date is now displayed in the transactions that display the accrual object, such as ACACTREE02 or POACTREE03.

Summary of Changes

· Pause Action and Status: A new Pause action and its corresponding status Paused have been introduced.

· Suspend Action Enhancements:

o The Suspend action now performs a release posting (an incompatible change).

o Requires the entry of a Suspension Date.

· Date Display: Both Pause and Suspension dates are displayed in relevant transactions like ACACTREE02 or POACTREE03.

These improvements provide greater flexibility and control over accrual subobject management, enhancing the overall usability of the system.

Purpose of Pause and Suspend Actions

The primary purpose of the Pause and Suspend actions is to prevent future additional accruals from being posted. These actions change the status of the accrual subobject to either Paused or Suspended.

Date Requirement

Both the Pause and Suspend actions require specifying a date from which the status Paused or Suspended becomes effective.

Differences Between Pause and Suspend

While both actions have a similar effect in preventing future accruals, there is a key difference between them:

Suspend:

o Triggers a release posting: After the Suspend action is performed, the accrual balance of the accrual subobject is set to zero.

o Intended for scenarios where the accrual balance should be zeroed out.

Pause:

o Does not trigger a release posting: The accrual balance remains as it is.

o Intended for scenarios where the accrual balance should be maintained.

Temporary Status Indication

Both Paused and Suspended are intended to be temporary statuses. The user typically plans to resume the accrual subobject at some later point in time.

Purpose: to prevent future additional accruals from being posted or temporarily halt the accrual process.

Final Status: Prematurely Finished

· Purpose:

o Permanently stop accruals; mark the accrual subobject as finished from a business standpoint.

o No further accruals should be posted.

· Status:

o Prematurely Finished: Triggers a release posting; the accrual balance is set to zero.

 

Status Impact on Utilization PostingsStatuses: Suspended and Prematurely Finished

· Accrual Balance:

o When the status of the accrual subobject is Suspended or Prematurely Finished, the accrual balance is set to zero.

o After the corresponding date (Suspension Date or Premature Finish Date), there are no remaining accruals.

o The remaining accruals are released when the status is changed.

Utilization Postings

· Key Point: Despite the accrual balance being zero, utilization postings can still occur.

o Scenario: If actual costs are posted with a posting date after the Suspension Date or Premature Finish Date, the system will handle these costs in a specific way.

o Handling: The corresponding amount of the release posting will be reversed, and this amount will be posted as utilization instead.

Higher Priority for Utilization

· Priority Rule: Utilization has higher priority than the release of accruals.

o Reasoning: The system regards the former release posting as a mistake. This is because it was later determined that the accruals were actually needed to cover actual costs.

o Correction Mechanism: The system automatically reverses the release posting and posts the amount as utilization to ensure that actual costs are appropriately covered.

Summary

Statuses and Their Implications:

· Suspended or Prematurely Finished:

o Accrual balance set to zero.

o No remaining accruals after the respective date.

o Former accruals are released.

Utilization Postings Despite Zero Balance:

· Condition: Actual costs posted after the Suspension Date or Premature Finish Date.

· System Action:

o Reverses the corresponding release posting.

o Posts the amount as utilization.

Priority Handling:

· Higher Priority for Utilization:

o The system considers the need for utilization over the release of accruals.

o Corrects the release posting to account for actual costs.

By understanding these rules and mechanisms, users can effectively manage accrual subobjects and ensure proper handling of actual costs and accrual balances. The system’s prioritization of utilization postings helps in accurately reflecting the actual financial state of the accrual subobjects.

entry.title: Reimagining Healthcare HR: How SAP Is Powering the Workforce of the Future
entry.content:
Healthcare providers around the world are navigating one of the most challenging workforce environments in modern history. Staffing shortages, clinician burnout, rising patient volumes, and increasingly complex compliance requirements are forcing hospitals and health systems to rethink how they attract, develop, and retain talent. At the same time, the global healthcare workforce management market is experiencing rapid growth—expected to rise from $1.9 billion in 2024 to $3.1 billion by 2029 as providers adopt digital tools to manage workforce complexity. (Source: businesswire)

Yet despite this momentum, many discussions overlook a critical truth: SAP is already a leading force in healthcare HR transformation. From cloud HR platforms to intelligent workforce planning, SAP enables providers to run more agile, data‑driven, and employee‑centric organizations.

Below is SAP’s powerful story in healthcare HR—now enhanced with a dedicated section on AI innovation in SAP SuccessFactors.

The Workforce Crisis Demands Modern HR Transformation

Hospitals face enormous pressure to do more with less. Workforce management tools are now essential for scheduling, time tracking, compliance, and credentialing—capabilities that underpin safe care delivery and workforce stability. Healthcare providers are deploying digital HR systems to reduce administrative burden and optimize staffing across hospitals, clinics, long-term care, and home care settings.

This makes a connected, intelligent HR ecosystem more essential than ever—and SAP is uniquely positioned to deliver it.

SAP: A Leading Force in Healthcare Workforce Management

SAP is consistently cited among top global vendors in the healthcare workforce management space, alongside Oracle, ADP and Workday.

Its portfolio supports the entire HR lifecycle, offering healthcare organizations a unified, scalable platform designed to improve efficiency and workforce engagement. Three strengths define SAP’s leadership:

1. SAP SuccessFactors: The Digital Core for Healthcare HR

SAP SuccessFactors provides end-to-end HR capabilities tailored for complex healthcare environments—from Employee Central and Time Management to Learning, Onboarding, Performance, and Succession.

A standout example is the National Healthcare Group (NHG) in Singapore, which modernized HR for 20,000+ employees using SAP SuccessFactors. Through a phased rollout, NHG improved mobility, data accuracy, and learning while integrating SAP Analytics Cloud for real-time workforce insights and SAP Build Work Zone for a centralized digital employee experienc . (Source: SAP)

2. Real-Time Insights to Support Better Workforce Decisions

SAP tools enable healthcare leaders to leverage advanced analytics and AI for staffing decisions, compliance monitoring, and workforce well‑being. Automating admin tasks, enhancing data security, and streamlining scheduling and resource use all support better patient care and smoother daily operations.

3. Proven Impact Across Global Healthcare Providers

Leading providers such as UnitedHealth Group and CVS Health rely on SAP S/4HANA, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SuccessFactors to manage HR, finance, supply chain, and operations—reinforcing SAP’s influence across the healthcare value chain.

AI‑Powered Transformation in SAP SuccessFactors

AI is reshaping HR across all industries—but in healthcare, where workforce strain is acute, AI has game‑changing potential. SAP SuccessFactors is rapidly evolving with embedded AI, generative AI, and SAP’s conversational copilot Joule, creating an intelligent HR ecosystem that helps healthcare providers operate with more insight, speed, and precision.

Below is a look at the most relevant AI capabilities for healthcare HR, based on the latest 2025–2026 updates.

1. Joule: The Conversational AI Copilot for HR

SAP’s generative AI assistant Joule is deeply embedded across SAP SuccessFactors to automate and simplify HR work. Joule provides role‑aware, conversational support for managers, HR teams, and employees, offering contextual recommendations, drafting content, and guiding decision‑making.

By 2025–2026, Joule will support:

  • Automated performance review summaries (SAP Discovery Center)
  • AI‑generated interview questions (SAP Discovery Center)
  • Drafting job descriptions (SAP Discovery Center)

For healthcare, Joule becomes a digital assistant that helps nurse managers, HR coordinators, and compliance teams move faster with fewer errors.

2. Skills Intelligence & Dynamic Workforce Planning

The next generation of SuccessFactors includes AI‑powered skills ontology, which automatically maps workforce skills, identifies gaps, and suggests development paths based on real-time data .

This is critical for healthcare, where:

  • Credentialing and licensing are essential
  • Skill gaps can impact patient safety
  • Upskilling and cross‑training reduce staffing shortages

AI continuously updates skill profiles based on training, experience, and activities—helping hospitals build future‑ready clinical and operational teams.

3. AI‑Driven Recruiting and Talent Matching

Premium AI features in SuccessFactors streamline healthcare recruiting—one of the industry's biggest challenges.

AI supports:

  • Skill extraction and matching from CVs
  • AI‑generated job postings tailored to target applicants
  • AI‑assisted candidate screening
  • Automated interview question generation

Hospitals can fill critical clinical roles faster while improving candidate quality and reducing administrative load on recruiting teams.

4. Personalized Learning & Development Through AI

Base AI in SuccessFactors already recommends learning content; Premium AI goes further by personalizing learning journeys and integrating contextual development opportunities.

This provides:

  • Tailored upskilling paths for clinicians
  • Automated career insights
  • AI-assisted guidance on role transitions

In healthcare, where compliance training is extensive and continuous skill development is mandatory, AI‑driven learning is a major productivity boost.

5. People Intelligence: Unified HR & Business Data for Smarter Decisions

SuccessFactors’ 2H 2025 release introduces People Intelligence, unifying HR and business data from SAP and external systems into a single view. With AI‑assisted insights powered by Joule and hundreds of pre‑built metrics, HR leaders can quickly identify workforce trends, risks, and opportunities .

For healthcare systems, this means:

  • Better modeling of patient‑to‑staff ratios
  • Faster insights into burnout and turnover risk
  • More accurate staffing and skill planning
  • Improved compliance reporting

This aligns workforce planning more tightly with operational and patient-care needs.

Conclusion: SAP Is the Future of Healthcare Workforce Transformation

As the healthcare workforce management market accelerates—with growth rates above 10% forecast well into 2035—providers need a strategic partner who understands the complexity of healthcare HR . SAP delivers exactly that.

With integrated HR, AI-powered insights, unified business data, and powerful digital tools, SAP empowers healthcare providers to:

  • Simplify HR operations
  • Optimize staffing decisions
  • Improve employee engagement and well‑being
  • Increase compliance and accuracy
  • Enhance patient care through a more supported workforce

SuccessFactors + AI represents the most advanced, scalable HR platform available for healthcare today—one capable of addressing workforce challenges with intelligence, automation, and human-centered design.

SAP’s story in healthcare HR is already strong. With AI, it becomes transformational.

entry.title: Re: Cancellation of billing invoice even it is referenced...
entry.content:
Hi @GARIMA1 

You can achieve this by implementing the logic in the user exit program RV60AFZC inside the USEREXIT_NUMBER_RANGE_INV_DATE form routine.

The approach is straightforward:

First, check the VBFA table to identify if the current Billing Invoice has any Credit Memo created with reference to it. Use VBFA-VBELV to match the Billing Document number and filter VBFA-VBTYP_N = 'O' (which represents a Credit Memo in the document flow).

If VBFA returns an entry, it confirms that a Credit Memo exists for this Billing Invoice. The next step is to verify whether that Credit Memo has already been reversed or not. For this, check the VBRK table using VBRK-VBELN = VBFA-VBELN and add the condition FKSTO NE 'X' — since FKSTO = 'X' indicates a cancelled document.

If VBRK returns an entry with this condition, it means the Credit Memo is still active and has not been cancelled. At this point, raise an error message to block the cancellation:

MESSAGE e000(ZSD) WITH 'Cannot cancel invoice' VBEK-VBELN '- Active Credit/Debit Memo exists:' WA_VBRK-VBELN.

This will prevent the user from cancelling the Billing Invoice until all referenced Credit Memos (or Debit Memos) are reversed first, ensuring document flow integrity and clean FI reconciliation.

Thanks,

Naveenkumar.K

entry.title: Dear Community,Public Cloud EWM: Automatic Return Task for remaining HU without Pick Point
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am looking for a solution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition EWM to handle the automatic return of remaining stock after picking.

Since, according to my knowledge, the standard Pick Point functionality is not available in the Public Cloud, I am trying to find the standard alternative or Best Practice to trigger the return process automatically.

My specific requirement is the following:

  1. A full pallet (HU) is moved to a work center / staging area for picking.

  2. After the required partial quantity is picked and the task is confirmed, I need the system to automatically generate a warehouse task (Return Task) for the remaining quantity to move the HU back to the storage area.

Since this is the Public Cloud version, how can I configure the system (e.g., via POSC or specific Work Center / Storage Type settings) to ensure that this return warehouse task is triggered automatically right after the pick confirmation, without manual intervention?

Any insights or best practice setup suggestions would be highly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

Best Regards,

Adam

entry.title: Re: VSCode - Unable to deploy or preview to ABAP on-prem...
entry.content: Hi, the error "EPROTO 40540000:error:0A0000C6:SSL" usually indicates that the client attempted to establish an HTTPS/TLS connection, but the remote endpoint responded with non-TLS traffic or an invalid TLS response i.e. it tried to connect to a non HTTPS endpoint! Update your YAML configurations to bypass SSL errors; ignoreCertErrors: true. For more info look at this git repo; https://github.com/SAP-samples/fiori-tools-samples/tree/main/misc/sslcerts.

entry.title: Activating Review and/or Approval for Periodic Accrual Amounts
entry.content:
Steps During Period End Closing

1. Execute the Accrual Proposal Run:

  •  Transaction: ACEPROPOSALRUN
  • Purpose: This step calculates and stores the accrual amounts for individual accrual objects in a database table. These accrual amounts represent the proposals that will be reviewed and/or approved.

2. Review and/or Approval Transactions:

a. For Reviewers:

  • Transaction: FACRARVWCO
  •  Users: Controllers (and optionally G/L accountants)
  • Authorization Check: Checks authorization to perform CO reporting on corresponding CO objects like cost center or profit center.
  •  Customization: Enhance authorization check logics if needed using the IMG activity Enhance Authorization Check Logics. Use the BAdI BADI_FACRA_AUTH_CHECK.
  •  Enhancement Spot: Selection parameters in the selection screen can be enhanced with the enhancement spot ES_FACRA_RVW_APPRV_SEL.

b. For Approvers:

  • Transaction: FACRAAPPRVGL
  •  Users: Primarily G/L accountants
  •  Enhancement Spot: Selection parameters in the selection screen can be enhanced with the enhancement spot ES_FACRA_APPROVAL_SEL.
  • BAdis Available for Both Transactions (FACRARVWCO and FACRAAPPRVGL):

i. BADI_FACRA_ADJUST_RESULT: Adjust the selection result before it is shown to the end user.

ii. BADI_FACRA_UI_NAVIGATION: Define the navigation target when clicking on a field in the worklist.

iii. BADI_FACRA_ADJMT_TOL_CHECK: Check whether the adjustment of the accrual amount is acceptable and whether an attachment is provided.

3. Execute the Accrual Posting Run:

  • Transaction: ACEPOSTINGRUN
  •  Purpose: Post the reviewed and/or approved accrual amounts.

Additional Features for Review and Approval

Define Thresholds: Set thresholds to avoid reviewing or approving small amounts, thus streamlining the process.

Define Time Intervals: Specify time intervals during which the review and approval processes must take place to ensure timely completion.
